frameworks.name: recurrent vs feedforward processing
frameworks.one_line: dissociates recurrent from feedforward contributions along the ventral visual stream

frameworks.name: backward masking paradigm
frameworks.one_line: masking used to isolate recurrent processing in EEG and fMRI

frameworks.name: convolutional neural network models
frameworks.one_line: CNN feature hierarchy benchmarks how recurrence shifts feature format

frameworks.name: spectro-temporal neural dynamics
frameworks.one_line: characterizes recurrence through theta-to-beta frequency components

core_question: Where, when, and how does recurrent processing shape object representations across the ventral visual stream?
key_assumptions: backward masking selectively disrupts recurrent processing
multivariate analysis can tease apart feedforward and recurrent components
CNN feature hierarchy provides a comparable representational benchmark
manipulations: presence vs absence of backward masking
comparison of brain representations against CNN layers
key_findings: recurrence shapes EVC representations early around 100 ms
two later recurrence phases emerge in LOC around 175 and 300 ms
recurrence adds persistent rather than transient neural dynamics
recurrence shifts LOC features from mid-level toward high-level
interpretations: recurrent processing substantially shapes ventral-stream visual representations
recurrence is mediated by four distinct spectro-temporal components spanning theta to beta
